sections.title: Talking to Dondakan after accepting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea on what's inside the rock?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, but that's where you come in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I want you to find out some more information on the rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now where would I do that?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I don't know! Wish I knew!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: But we have to get some better understanding of what this thing actually is. Maybe then we can find a way to break it open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what are you going to do while I go running across half of Gielinor, I'll wager?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I'll guard my cannon and fire more cannonballs at it, of course! Wish I had your job, mine's a tough one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Want to trade places?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: My legs are too old and worn to do much running around anymore, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, off you go now! Start running, start talking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I still don't know where!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, why so difficult?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Look, try the city, further down the stream here. There's bound to be someone there who knows something. If you still can't find anyone, go to the librarian, he knows a lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I want you to find out some more information on the rock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now where would I do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Welcome to the Keldagrim library, human traveller!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about impenetrable rocks?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: You're confusing me, what rock exactly are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The big one in the mines south of the city. Dondakan's big rock.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I see, I see.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: But I don't know much about that rock, I'm afraid. It's baffled us for many generations.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: There is a dwarf in the mechanics shop, though, who I believe has some connections with a certain scholar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find him exactly?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Go south to the palace, the shop is called Wemund's Wrench Warehouse. He should be working there somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about this rock in the mines?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Now what rock would that be, exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The big rock in the mines to the south, with an old dwarf standing beside it..?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Ah, Don's rock, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yes, I think so. Do you know what it's made of?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: No, no one knows. No one here, anyway. The only one I can think of is Rolad, a renowned scholar. He was part of Dondakan's team once, many years ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where can I find this Rolad?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: He left to explore the world. Learning by experience, or so he said. Me, I prefer a cold stout at home, but it takes all sorts.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: I think he's currently with the Black Guard at Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks a lot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me... would you happen to be Rolad the scholar?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Why, yes, I think so... who's asking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name], and I've come to ask you about a rock.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: You're awfully tall for a dwarf, you know, [player name]. And a rock you say? I suppose I know a fair bit about rocks, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's this rock that Dondakan is trying to break open. You know Dondakan, right?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh, old Dondakan, sure I know him. And I know his rock too. It's that unbreakable one, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's the one. What can you tell me about it?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Well, as a matter of fact, I picked up an interesting book on one of my travels, from a quite out of the way dwarven outpost.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: I'm not entirely sure, but it seems to describe this very rock, and how it came to be placed in that cavern. I tore out the most interesting pages for more detailed perusal. Let me get them for you.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh dear...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: I seem to have misplaced the pages... a pity, I had wanted to study them some more. Oh well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? No! I need that book!

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh, well, if it's that important to you, perhaps you can go and look for the pages? There should be three of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where should I start looking?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: I probably lost them in the mines below. I don't have them up here in any case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, in the mining guild?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: No no, I never go there, I'm not a member you know. But I sometimes go down to the regular mines to study the conditions of the working dwarf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks, I'll go and look for the pages then.

sections.title: Talking to Dondakan after learning about the book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rolad has a book that might help us!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: A book? How's a book going to help? What's in it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's the problem... the most important pages are missing.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Then run along and find them!

sections.title: Hi ho, hi ho, now where'd those pages go?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: How are you getting on with finding those missing pages?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found a single one yet, I have no idea where I should be looking.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Down in the mines below, that'd be my best bet. I don't know where though, they could be anywhere down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I found the first page, but I still have two to go.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: That's splendid, keep going and you'll find them all, I'm sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found two of the pages! But I haven't found the last one yet.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Marvelous! I'm sure it's only a matter of time before you find the final page!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I found all of your missing pages!

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Missing pages..?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, the missing pages of that book we were talking about earlier?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh yes, quite right... let me see them, please, and I'll put the book back together for you.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: There you go... You will give it back when you've finished with it, won't you? I'd really like to study it in more detail some time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, I'd be happy to!

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Splendid, now, there's a good dwarf!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course... when I've finished with it I'll give it back to you, sure...

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Splendid, now, there's a good dwarf!

sections.title: Talking to Rolad before reading the book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks again for that book, I really appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh yes, have you read it yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet, but I will.

sections.title: If the player loses the book before reading it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahem... errr... I lost that book you gave me.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: That's terrible! Have you even read it yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I hadn't had the chance yet.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Well, here, I happened to have a copy of it. Try not to lose it this time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, if you had a copy of it, why did I have to look for your missing pages?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: I suppose I just forgot that I had one.

sections.title: Talking to Rolad after reading the book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was quite an interesting book you gave me.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh yes, have you read it yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I'd like to hang on to it a little longer, if I may.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Why, naturally, that's no problem for someone with such a keen interest in expanding [his/her/their] knowledge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I said hello!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Why, I'm Dondakan, of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you firing a cannon at a wall?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: It's not a wall! It's a rock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the difference?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: It's a very special rock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still don't quite follow...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, for Guthix' sake, it's perfectly simple! See, that rock over there, it's impenetrable!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you tried taking a pickaxe to it?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Of course I have, I'm holding this rune pickaxe, aren't I? We've been trying to mine through it for years!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We? I don't see anyone else.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, I'm the only one left... all the others gave up. Said it wasn't worth their while anymore. Wouldn't have a good return profit on their time investment.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: At first we thought there might be a huge treasure behind the rock, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't there?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Until we had a look further down the stream, around the rock... no, there's no treasure there, just a dead end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... so why were you trying to get through the rock again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Because obviously there are vast deposits of minerals inside the rock! And if not, I want to harness the extreme hardness of the rock itself!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: The only problem is that I have run out of ideas on how to do it.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Perhaps a young adventurer like you is willing to help out an old dwarf like me? Want to find out what's inside the rock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, sounds interesting!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Welcome aboard then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I do enough mining on my own.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Suit yourself, I don't need any help from a human anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bit noisy here, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Cozy? No, not really, it's damp and cold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! I said it's noisy!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Then stop shouting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rolad gave me a book that was quite interesting to read! I think I know what's inside it!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What? What? Tell me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ehm, well... a demonic spirit for one...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: A what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A demonic spirit! And apparently the inside of the rock is lined with gold!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Gold? Did you say gold?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... and a de-

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Gold! I knew it! I struck gold! I'm set for life!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What's left of it anyway...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Now tell me, how do I break open the rock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, the book doesn't say... it doesn't seem to be particularly concerned with actually breaking open the rock. You see, the demonic spirit is being kept inside it.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Yes yes, I heard you the first time.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, we're not very much closer to a solution then, are we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you tried so far?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, let's see...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: At one time, we had a hundred dwarves in here, all picking away at the rock. Didn't do a thing.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Then there was the time when we set fire to this whole section of the cave. What a smell that made!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any effect?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Apart from coating the rock with soot? Not really, no.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I was able to borrow a cannon from the Black Guard, my son was able to pull some strings you know.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Of course, steel cannonballs didn't make a dent. But then ol' Donnie had a clever idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I made this adamantite cannonball, you see! Cost me quite a pile of coins too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! That must have made quite an impact!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, yes, it did... when it bounced back onto the ground it did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What else did you try?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, then I had an even more clever idea!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I put an even bigger pile of coins together to get some rune cannonballs! Good idea, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, great!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, it wasn't. Didn't do anything! Nothing at all!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Latest thing I've tried are these boots.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice boots... but what about them?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: They're made out of granite! I don't have enough money left to make granite cannonballs though. But see!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Did you see? Did you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I er... didn't see anything.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: That's because there wasn't anything to see! Even granite doesn't do anything to it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And now I have to come up with a material that will affect it somehow?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: You got it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some kind of new material that no one has ever found before but which I am supposed to accidentally stumble upon, perhaps?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Don't be silly, I'm sure there must be some known material that will affect it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm tired of these stories.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Go on then... leave an old dwarf to his work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe we should set up another cannon?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Another cannon? How will that help?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, no, what we need is a different material of cannonballs. The hardest, toughest you can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean, that I can find? What are you doing then?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I'm guarding the cannon. Won't do us much good if it were stolen, would it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

sections.title: Using different metal ores/bars on Dondakan

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Tried that, didn't work.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with that?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Perfect gold? That's much too valuable to be used to make cannonballs!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Haha, you want me to make a golden cannonball?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: You know that apart from being heavy it's also very soft, don't you? It'd be a pointless waste!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... the book does say that there's gold inside the rock, so I thought perhaps gold can damage it as well.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Hmm, I suppose you have a point there.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Tell you what, if you make a golden cannonball and give it to me, I'll fire it at the rock.

sections.title: Using a gold ore/bar on Dondakan again

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Yes, you already showed me that. But I'm not going to waste my own gold on it, make a cannonball and bring it to me.

sections.title: Using gold bar on a furnace with an ammo mould
Using the golden cannonball on Dondakan

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that golden cannonball you asked me to make.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: That... that... that's an amazing looking cannonball!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Are you sure you want me to fire it at the rock? It's going to make a nasty dent in it. In the cannonball that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm sure this will crack open the rock.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: It breaks my heart to ruin such a fine piece of crafting, but it's your gold... Alright, let's load it up then.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Did... did... did you see that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The cannonball went right through it!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I don't believe it! All this time, I've been spending all my gold on opening the rock... I could've just thrown it at it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... the rock didn't actually break, did it?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, it seemed more like it... gave way for a second... That's interesting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how does this help us?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Why, I'll just shoot you through it so you can have a look, won't I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shoot me... through it? Errr... what exactly do you mean?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Very simple. I'll have to make a few modifications to my cannon so that it can fire a human, and you'll need a golden helmet of course.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Then I'll fire you at the rock so you can do a little reconnaissance. Don't worry, you should land safely on the other side of the rock.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Might want to pick up that cannonball again while you're at it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... what if I bump into that demon, the one from the book?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Don't be silly now. It's just a rock... just a rock with lots of gold in it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want to... fire me into the rock?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Yes, just to see what's inside... to see if all that gold is really embedded inside it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure that it's safe?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, but then we'll never know until we've tried it, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Find someone else for your bizarre experiment, I'm not going to crawl into that cannon of yours! Or have you shoot me at a solid rock!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: But we're so close to all that gold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't argue with that, shoot me in!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, it's not so simple... like I said, I need to make some modifications to my cannon. They're not really designed to fire humans, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll just wait right here until you've made them.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I don't really know how, actually. Never been much of an engineer.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: If I had the right schematics for it, I could probably work with that. But I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so you want me to look for the schematics for you, is that it?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: That's it, yes! Here, I have one part of the schematics, sent to me by some prankster dwarf, I don't even remember his name. You'll have to find the rest on your own.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, and you'll also need a golden helmet of course. Would hurt quite a bit otherwise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Won't a gold plated helmet do?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, it has to be a helmet made out of solid gold. Gold plated helmets contain so little actual gold, it'd be a bit dangerous.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Unless you want to die when I shoot you at the rock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No no, I'll get you a helmet made out of solid gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Although that will be a horrendously heavy thing to actually wear.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Yes yes, well, get going now, why don't you? I'll guard the cannon while you're away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I thought you'd say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My head's spinning, I have to go for a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, on second thought, I'd rather sell it for a huge profit.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Nice idea, [player name], but no one will buy a golden cannonball!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: It looks nice, but it's not much use as a projectile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to find again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Schematics to modify my cannon, and a golden helmet for you to wear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that schematic you gave me, actually.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, be more careful! Here, have a copy, but don't lose it again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds simple enough. Any idea on where I can find those schematics?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Not a clue! I don't even know how many parts there are. It's probably best to ask any dwarven engineers that you might know.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: And it could be that someone in the mines or the city here knows where this part came from. Didn't you talk to someone in Keldagrim already? Just ask around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No complaining now, run along!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for schematics to modify the dwarven multicannon to fire a human. Can you help?

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Haha, yesh, I have jusht the shing!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: I've been working on thish deshign for quite a while now!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Never got to test it, love to shee it shoot a real human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, can you give them to me? I'd be willing to try the experiment.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Oooh, shure, shure... I've only got one part left, though, I gave all the other parts away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who did you give them to?

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: How should I know? I'm only a shimple dwarf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, can I have that one schematic then?

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Of courshe!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Just give me a drink and I'll gladly help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've had enough of buying drinks for people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's always like this... Sure, I'll help you [player name], just as soon as you get me a drink!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And more often than not they actually want a very special kind of drink that can only be gotten in the next town, or beyond.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is wrong with all you people anyway? Sometimes it seems as if half the population of Gielinor are alcoholics!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, no, I won't do it anymore, you can get your own drink for once, and I'm just going to stay right here until you've done so!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Pffft, I only wanted a dwarven shtout from that table over there.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Here, have the shtupid schematic, I've gone right off my drink now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, alright, what do you want to drink?

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Oooh, jusht a glash of dwarven shtout over there...

sections.title: Using a dwarven stout on Khorvak

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, I've got your dwarven stout.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: That'sh wonderful! You're a good human, you are. Here, have thish schematic and have fun with it!

sections.title: Talking to Khorvak after receiving his schematic

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for that schematic.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Sho have you been shot out of a cannon yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, fortunately not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been so helpful with that book, I was wondering, can you help me with something else as well?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Of course, anything to help a fellow dwarf, especially such a big, strong one such as yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dondakan wants to modify his multicannon, you see, to fire a human.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: A human, you say? Are we using humans as cannon fodder now?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Well, good for him, never did like humans anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But can you help me with the schematics? Dondakan needs to know what he needs to modify.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh, I see, I see. No, I have no schematics to... what did you say? Modify the cannon to shoot humans? Such a preposterous idea.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: No, all I have are the base schematics of the cannon. I was on the multicannon project once, you know?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: You see, back when-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, those will be a tremendous help already, can I borrow those off of you as well?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Well, of course, I have the schematics written down on a piece of paper somewhere. Let me just look for it.

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh dear, I seem to have lost the schematics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are rather a forgetful person, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: I hardly ever lose anything, I'll have you know!

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Now, you wouldn't be able to go and look for the schematics, would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, alright, I'll go and have a look.

sections.title: Talking to Rolad while having lost the dwarven lore book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Say, you wouldn't happen to have another copy of that book, would you?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Don't tell me you lost that book!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yes, I lost your book...

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Lucky that I have another copy, isn't it? Please don't lose it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks a lot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't seem to find your schematics... Did you lose them down in the mines again?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh, let's see... well... maybe. I'm not altogether sure where I left them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No idea at all?

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Sorry, no... I do not even remember what I drew them on.

sections.title: Talking to Rolad after finding the schematic

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found your schematics! You wrote them down on one of the pages of that book you gave me!

lines.speaker: Rolad
lines.text: Oh, of course, why didn't I think of that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any schematics related to the dwarven multicannon?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Why, what do you need them for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so you do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dondakan needs schematics to modify his multicannon, to fire a human you see.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: And you'd be the test subject? I mean, the cannonball?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ehm, yes, I suppose so...

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Haha, that I would like to see!

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Here, take this. Khorvak sent it to me from White Wolf Mountain, kind of as a joke. It's incomplete, of course, doesn't mean anything without other parts.

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Haha, he's always a laugh, that Khorvak. Shooting humans out of cannons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any schematics related to the dwarven multicannon?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: You wouldn't be able to carry them anyway, so why ask for them?

sections.title: Talking to the Dwarven Engineer again with the schematic

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any schematics related to the dwarven multicannon?

lines.speaker: Dwarven Engineer
lines.text: Yes, and I already gave a part to you, remember?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have all the different parts of the schematics...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Let me have a look at that!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: This doesn't make any sense, it's all jumbled up! Sort it out, will you, don't torment an old dwarf with nonsensical drawings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that golden helmet you said I'd need, what else did you need again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I still need those schematics to modify my cannon. It doesn't normally fire humans, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have all the different parts of the schematics...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Let me have a look at that!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: This doesn't make any sense, it's all jumbled up! Sort it out, will you, don't torment an old dwarf with nonsensical drawings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that golden helmet you said I'd need, what else did you need again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I still need those schematics to modify my cannon. It doesn't normally fire humans, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds simple enough. Any idea on where I can find those schematics?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Not a clue! I don't even know how many parts there are. It's probably best to ask any dwarven engineers that you might know.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: And it could be that someone in the mines or the city here knows where this part came from. Didn't you talk to someone in Keldagrim already? Just ask around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No complaining now, run along!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your schematics... what else was I supposed to do?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: You'll need a golden helmet as well, if you want to survive the blast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would prefer that, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that golden helmet you said I'd need, what else did you need again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I still need those schematics to modify my cannon. It doesn't normally fire humans, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that schematic you gave me, actually.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, be more careful! Here, have a copy, but don't lose it again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that golden helmet you said I'd need, what else did you need again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I still need those schematics to modify my cannon. It doesn't normally fire humans, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds simple enough. Any idea on where I can find those schematics?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Not a clue! I don't even know how many parts there are. It's probably best to ask any dwarven engineers that you might know.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: And it could be that someone in the mines or the city here knows where this part came from. Didn't you talk to someone in Keldagrim already? Just ask around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No complaining now, run along!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your schematics all assembled, and I have that golden helmet as well!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, go ahead and wear your helmet! Won't protect your head if it's not on your head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your schematics all assembled, and I have that golden helmet as well!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Wonderful! These schematics look simple enough... are you ready for your quick trip through the rock then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You may fire when ready!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Ah, such enthusiasm! Okay, here we go, let me have a look at those schematics and we'll get started.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I'm sorry, you will have to wait a moment, this schematic is more complex than I thought.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Right, sitting comfortably?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, not really, no...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Excellent, excellent, I'll get the cannon ready to fire then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm starting to get second thoughts about this!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Just think of all that gold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm thinking of that demon...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Don't worry, it'll all be over in a moment!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I'm afraid of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where... where am I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dondakan said there was supposed to be a river behind the rock... but I don't see a river here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think... I think... I think I may be...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...inside the rock!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: You are in my domain now! Begone lest you wish to be destroyed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow, my poor head... this helmet seems heavier than ever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready as I'll ever be...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Okay, here we go, let me have a look at those schematics and we'll get started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, maybe another time, I have some important business to attend to.

sections.title: Talking to Dondakan after being shot into the mine

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: So, tell me! What did you see inside! Is there any gold in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... gold, yes...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Haha, wonderful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I ehm, also ran into this spirit thing. You remember, from the book?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Ran into him? How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The rock... seems to be a lot larger than it appears on the outside. I was actually able to walk around in there. But it was like... walking around in a dream.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: And this spirit you speak of? Was it guarding the gold?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite sure, it all seems so distant now.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well, if it is, that must mean you must kill it! Simple!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Kill it. Simple. Okay.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: So are you ready to have another go in the rock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready as I'll ever be...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Not without wearing your golden helmet you're not!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Okay, here we go, hold on to your helmet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, maybe another time, I have some important business to attend to.

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: Stop what you're doing or taste my wrath!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: Do not touch what is not yours!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: I would not do that again if I were you!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: You are bringing doom down on yourself!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: You did not heed my messages to stay away! You have brought doom down on to yourself!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: There are many aspects to my being and there are many skills I possess.

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: You, I can see, are a skilled warrior. Yet your might is no match for my magic!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: You are an able ranger, yet that is useless against my might!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: Your magical abilities are impressive, but my powers of archery will foil them!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: Behold, as my avatar descends to your level to extinguish the flame of your puny existence!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: I am invincible! You stand no chance against me!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: You are no match for me!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Being of Bordanzan
lines.text: And while your power is great, mortal, I will still resist you!

sections.title: Attacking the avatar while having no gold in your inventory

lines.speaker: Arzinian Avatar of [Magic/Ranging/Strength]
lines.text: Your puny efforts cannot harm me!

lines.speaker: Arzinian Avatar of [Magic/Ranging/Strength]
lines.text: Give up and resign yourself to your death!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: The whole cave was shaking! What happened in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I em... let me just collect my thoughts. It all seems like a sort of distant dream now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I killed the Arzinian demon thing.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What, you actually killed him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I killed its avatar. I'm not sure what happened to the demon itself.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Probably banished it to some hellish realm or other.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: He'll be back, but not for a few thousand years, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: But in the mean time, there's plenty of gold for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For us.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Of course, of course! Whenever you want to go back in there, let me know... just don't forget to bring your helmet!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: And here, have this rune pickaxe as well, I can just use a normal one now anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks a lot!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No no, thank you! After all these years, the riddle of the rock has finally been cracked!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing, Dondakan?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, just fine, just fine! This gold is certainly creating a steady flow of income for me! After the company takes some of the cut, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you shoot me into the rock again?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: You need to wear that golden helmet... unless you want to crack open your skull on the rock.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Of course, just a second, let me adjust my cannon again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Say, are you selling those boots of yours?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What, you mean these granite boots?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'd be happy to pay quite some money for them!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Haha, I bet you would, I bet you would!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: But I no longer need the money, this rock is generating plenty!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Besides, I'm rather fond of them now... best boots I ever had!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh please...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No, I won't sell them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty please?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on!!!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: How much gold would you be willing to pay for it? Not that I'm selling my boots, mind, I'm just curious.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: What, are you trying to scam old Dondakan? You want me to sell these boots for a measly [number] coins?

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I'm not selling them anyway.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: You're so poor, you don't even have that much gold on you...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: If I were selling my granite boots, which I'm clearly not, I would certainly not accept a mere [number] coins for them.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: That's quite a sum of money, which I notice you don't even have on you.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Haha, nice try [player name]!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Pity I'm not selling my boots, not for any price or any item.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: I just wanted to see how high you would go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bah, thanks a lot.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Hey, that's quite a bit of money. Which you don't have on you. Oh well, I wasn't selling my boots anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I don't want them anymore.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Great, because I don't want to sell them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Check these out.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Where'd you get those from? Did you make your own?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite, they were retrieved from the corpse of a wyvern.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: You killed a wyvern? Last I heard they were all dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I never said I killed it! But perhaps I did, I am a grand adventure after all!

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Oh dear, oh dear. It seems firing you head first into a rock was perhaps not the best idea, you've gone absolutely loony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, well you do have a point...

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: It's alright Laddie, I'm just joking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great, but I have to go now.

lines.speaker: Dondakan the Dwarf
lines.text: Come back any time!

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Welcome to the Keldagrim library, human traveller!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about impenetrable rocks?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: You're confusing me, what rock exactly are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The big one in the mines south of the city. Dondakan's big rock.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I see, I see.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I heard there was a demon inside there that you banished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, what exactly does that mean anyway?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: There are some demons that we cannot see properly as mortals. They also cannot be fought with normal weapons.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: But sometimes these creatures manifest themselves in a solid form in our world. Their avatar.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: When you defeat an avatar, you defeat the actual demon behind it. But they don't die, they are banished to where they came from originally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that mean I will have to deal with this demon again some time?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Doubtful. He will grow stronger and stronger over time, but that's a process that will take a thousand years at least.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Even dwarves don't live so long.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: No, we won't see him again and the mines are safe to dig for gold now. Of course, that might lead to other problems eventually...
